channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Euro Truck Simulator 2 Soul of Anatolia DLC Takes You to Türkiye
item.content:encoded:
SCS Software has announced a new Euro Truck Simulator 2 DLC pack, and this one is taking you to the Anatolia peninsula, where you'll find the beautiful landscape of Türkiye.

Revealed just a few months after the game hit a new player count record on Steam, Soul of Anatolia doesn't have a release date or a release window yet, but when it lands, the DLC will let you transport cargo "from the bustling streets of Istanbul all the way to Aksaray".

Naturally, the capital city of Ankara will be represented, and you'll also be able to drive across landmarks like the Çanakkale Bridge, which still claims the title of the longest suspension bridge in the world.

It's not just Istanbul, either; you can also explore other Turkish cities like Antalya and Alanya, and you'll need to do so if you want to make sure your deliveries get where they need to go.

Of course, Türkiye is a very geographically varied country as well, so you can expect some "challenging" mountain roads complete with plenty of steep twists and turns. You'd better be on your guard if you want to avoid losing all of your cargo to the Turkish countryside.

The Soul of Anatolia DLC follows on from other popular Euro Truck Simulator expansions that featured regions like the Nordics (although not Russia, notably, since the game's Heart of Russia DLC was put on hold indefinitely in 2022 in response to the Russian invasion of Ukraine).

Much like some of those packs did, this one might take a while to be released; SCS Software says there's "a long way ahead" until the DLC is released, emphasizing that what's been shown so far is very much a work in progress.

Euro Truck Simulator 2's Soul of Anatolia DLC is available for wishlisting right now on Steam. You can check the base game out there as well.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
